summary: Iteration 172 comprehensive verification: All 104 legislation framework tests pass (93 legislation + 12 anti-fallback + 11 forensic language filter). All 8 states/territories return correct state-specific legislation without NSW contamination. Forensic language filter correctly rewrites over-assertive phrases. NSW case grounds contain substantive legislation (Crimes Act 1900) separate from appellate_pathway (Criminal Appeal Act). WA case grounds have no NSW contamination. Refresh Legal Refs endpoint works. Frontend displays Grounds tab with separate law_sections and appellate_pathway fields.
critical_code_review_comments: All 8 states/territories (NSW, VIC, QLD, SA, WA, TAS, NT, ACT) return correct state-specific legislation via /api/offence-framework endpoint
No NSW defaults remain in submit.py, extract.py, timeline.py, pipeline.py, compare.py, argue.py
Forensic language filter (enforce_forensic_language) correctly rewrites: 'The trial judge erred' → 'It is arguable that the trial judge erred'
Forensic language filter preserves precedent citations and already-forensic language
NSW case (case_6cc234434cbd) grounds have law_sections with Crimes Act 1900 (NSW) s 23A
WA case (case_e8a9de2d8331) grounds have no NSW contamination in law_sections
Appellate pathway (Criminal Appeal Act) is correctly separated from substantive law_sections
Refresh Legal Refs endpoint POST /api/cases/{case_id}/grounds/refresh-legal-refs returns 200 with updated/total counts

success_rate.frontend: 100% (Grounds tab displays correctly with separate law_sections and appellate_pathway)

seed_data_creation: None - used existing NSW case (case_6cc234434cbd) and WA case (case_e8a9de2d8331)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 172 verified comprehensive legislation framework: (1) All 104 tests pass including 93 legislation framework tests, 12 anti-NSW-fallback tests, and 11 forensic language filter tests. (2) All 8 states return correct state-specific legislation via /api/offence-framework endpoint - VIC returns Crimes Act 1958, QLD returns Criminal Code 1899, WA returns Criminal Code 1913, etc. (3) No NSW contamination in any non-NSW state responses. (4) Forensic language filter rewrites over-assertive phrases like 'The trial judge erred' to 'It is arguable that the trial judge erred'. (5) NSW case grounds have substantive law_sections (Crimes Act 1900 s 23A) separate from appellate_pathway (Criminal Appeal Act 1912). (6) WA case grounds have no NSW contamination. (7) Refresh Legal Refs endpoint works correctly.
